steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: "Conversely" signals a direct opposite. The sentence contrasts "historically significant" with "relatively ______". The blank must mean the opposite of historically significant — something like unimportant or of little consequence.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean unimportant or of little historical consequence. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — inconsequential: means having no significant effect or importance — directly opposite to "historically significant," making it the perfect contrast. ✓
  • Choice B — benevolent: means kind or generous — describes moral character, not historical importance; benevolence is not the opposite of significance. ✗
  • Choice C — genuine: means authentic or real — describes truthfulness, not historical impact; authenticity is not contrasted with significance. ✗
  • Choice D — momentous: means of great importance — the same meaning as "historically significant"; this reinforces the first term rather than contrasting it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
